edges.description: Reduced M-current decreases subthreshold K+ conductance and lowers the threshold for repetitive firing in neonatal cortical neurons, producing transient network hyperexcitability.
edges.source: KCNQ2/KCNQ3 K+ Channel Loss-of-Function and M-Current Attenuation

edges.description: Developmentally restricted cortical hyperexcitability manifests as neonatal seizures.
Network-level hyperexcitability manifests as focal neonatal seizures.
Neonatal network hyperexcitability can present with focal tonic semiology.
Neonatal network hyperexcitability can present with focal clonic motor semiology.
Focal neonatal seizures can secondarily generalize into tonic seizures.
Focal neonatal seizures can include autonomic features.
Ictal autonomic involvement can produce apnea during neonatal seizures.
Apneic ictal events can produce circumoral cyanosis.
Hyperexcitable neonatal motor networks produce clonic movements.
Hyperexcitable neonatal motor networks can produce limb myoclonic jerks.
Focal neonatal seizure activity is visible as focal EEG discharges that may secondarily generalize.
edges.target: Focal EEG Discharges with Secondary Generalization

nodes.description: Apnea and cyanosis frequently accompany ictal events in neonatal KCNQ2/3 epilepsy.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002104 | Apnea | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: A Distinctive Ictal Amplitude-Integrated Electroencephalography Pattern in Newborns with Neonatal Epilepsy Associated with KCNQ2 Mutations.
evidence.snippet: Refractory seizures occurred in the early neonatal period with similar seizure type, including tonic features, apnea, and desaturation.

nodes.description: Carbamazepine, a sodium-channel-blocking antiseizure medication, is effective for KCNQ2/KCNQ3-related neonatal seizures and is recommended as first- or second-line targeted therapy in this group.

evidence.reference_title: Distinctive Amplitude-Integrated EEG Ictal Pattern and Targeted Therapy with Carbamazepine in KCNQ2 and KCNQ3 Neonatal Epilepsy: A Case Series.
evidence.snippet: prompted the use of CBZ that was effective in all.

evidence.reference_title: A Distinctive Ictal Amplitude-Integrated Electroencephalography Pattern in Newborns with Neonatal Epilepsy Associated with KCNQ2 Mutations.
evidence.snippet: Prompt recognition of this pattern led to early treatment with carbamazepine in the 2 most recent cases.

nodes.description: Circumoral cyanosis accompanies ictal events at frequent rate, reflecting apneic and autonomic involvement in KCNQ2/KCNQ3-related neonatal seizures.

evidence.snippet: HP:0032556 | Circumoral cyanosis | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Clonic motor features occur at frequent rate in neonates with KCNQ2/KCNQ3-related epilepsy.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002169 | Clonus | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Focal seizures with autonomic features (e.g., apnea, cyanosis, heart-rate changes) occur at frequent rate in KCNQ2/KCNQ3-related neonatal epilepsy.

evidence.snippet: HP:0011154 | Focal autonomic seizure | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Focal clonic seizures, classically migrating between limbs and hemispheres, are commonly observed.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002266 | Focal clonic seizure | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Ictal EEG typically shows focal discharges that can secondarily generalize.
nodes.id: Focal EEG Discharges with Secondary Generalization

evidence.snippet: HP:0011188 | Focal EEG discharges with secondary generalization | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Brief focal tonic seizures, often with apnea and desaturation, are a characteristic ictal semiology in KCNQ2/KCNQ3 neonatal epilepsy.

evidence.snippet: HP:0011167 | Focal tonic seizure | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: A Distinctive Ictal Amplitude-Integrated Electroencephalography Pattern in Newborns with Neonatal Epilepsy Associated with KCNQ2 Mutations.
evidence.snippet: Refractory seizures occurred in the early neonatal period with similar seizure type, including tonic features, apnea, and desaturation.

nodes.description: Seizures are typically focal in onset, often with tonic or clonic motor features and may secondarily generalize.

evidence.snippet: HP:0007359 | Focal-onset seizure | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Tonic seizures with secondary generalization occur at frequent rate in neonates with KCNQ2/KCNQ3-related epilepsy, distinct from the focal tonic semiology that dominates initial presentations.

evidence.snippet: HP:0010818 | Generalized tonic seizure | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: A novel potassium channel gene, KCNQ2, is mutated in an inherited epilepsy of newborns.
evidence.snippet: Five other BFNC probands were shown to have KCNQ2 mutations, including two transmembrane missense mutations, two frameshifts and one splice-site mutation.
KCNQ2 | potassium voltage-gated channel subfamily Q member 2 | hgnc:6296 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

nodes.description: KCNQ2 (Kv7.2) and KCNQ3 (Kv7.3) subunits heteromultimerize to form the neuronal M-channel, a voltage-gated potassium channel that carries the slowly activating, non-inactivating M-current. The M-current acts in the subthreshold voltage range to dampen repetitive firing and constrain neuronal excitability. Heterozygous loss-of-function variants in KCNQ2 or KCNQ3 reduce M-current density.
nodes.id: KCNQ2/KCNQ3 K+ Channel Loss-of-Function and M-Current Attenuation

evidence.reference_title: KCNQ2 and KCNQ3 potassium channel subunits: molecular correlates of the M-channel.
evidence.snippet: It is concluded that both these subunits contribute to the native M-current.

evidence.reference_title: KCNQ2 and KCNQ3 potassium channel subunits: molecular correlates of the M-channel.
evidence.snippet: The M-current regulates the subthreshold electrical excitability of many neurons, determining their firing properties and responsiveness to synaptic input.

evidence.reference_title: A novel potassium channel gene, KCNQ2, is mutated in an inherited epilepsy of newborns.
evidence.snippet: This finding in BFNC provides additional evidence that defects in potassium channels are involved in the mammalian epilepsy phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: A pore mutation in a novel KQT-like potassium channel gene in an idiopathic epilepsy family.
evidence.snippet: We screened KCNQ3 for mutations in the large BFNC family previously linked to chromosome 8q24 in the same marker interval. We found a missense mutation in the critical pore region in perfect co-segregation with the BFNC phenotype.
KCNQ3 | potassium voltage-gated channel subfamily Q member 3 | hgnc:6297 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

nodes.description: Myoclonic jerks of the limbs occur at frequent rate in neonates with KCNQ2/KCNQ3-related epilepsy, distinct from focal clonic semiology.

evidence.snippet: HP:0045084 | Limb myoclonus | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Reduced M-current produces a mild, developmentally restricted state of cortical hyperexcitability that manifests as focal neonatal seizures. Because compensatory potassium conductances and inhibitory circuits mature over the first months of life, the hyperexcitability is transient and seizures spontaneously remit, distinguishing this benign phenotype from KCNQ2-related developmental and epileptic encephalopathy.

evidence.reference_title: ILAE Genetic Literacy Series: Self-limited familial epilepsy syndromes with onset in neonatal age and infancy.
evidence.snippet: Seizures tend to remit during infancy or early childhood and are therefore called "self-limited".

nodes.description: Seizures with neonatal onset are the defining clinical feature; first seizures typically occur within the first week of life.

evidence.snippet: HP:0032807 | Neonatal seizure | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: ILAE Genetic Literacy Series: Self-limited familial epilepsy syndromes with onset in neonatal age and infancy.
evidence.snippet: autosomal dominant disorders characterized by neonatal- or infantile-onset focal motor seizures and the absence of neurodevelopmental complications.

nodes.description: Sodium-channel blockers as a class (e.g., carbamazepine, oxcarbazepine, phenytoin) are favored over phenobarbital for KCNQ2/KCNQ3-related neonatal seizures based on a precision-medicine approach.

evidence.reference_title: A Distinctive Ictal Amplitude-Integrated Electroencephalography Pattern in Newborns with Neonatal Epilepsy Associated with KCNQ2 Mutations.
evidence.snippet: Early recognition of the electroclinical phenotype by using aEEG may direct genetic testing and a precision medicine approach with sodium channel blockers in neonates with KCNQ2 mutations.
